mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound, the discount rate applied to equity cash flows remains structurally compressed, supporting a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as investors reach for yield in risk assets.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window during the ZIRP period, or returns are not significantly higher than the full-sample mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in UNRATE of more than 0.2 percentage points over a two-month window signals accelerating labor market healing, which raises expected consumer income and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE fell to 8.9% in Feb 2011 from higher prior readings
failure_condition: Among triggered instances, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in less than 55% of cases, or rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS shows monthly job gains alongside rising INDPRO, the joint signal of expanding employment and industrial output confirms a broad-based real economy recovery not decomposable from either series alone, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month as earnings expectations are revised upward across sectors.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 0 AND INDPRO monthly change > 0 concurrently
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130515k and INDPRO at 95.14 as of early 2011 showing gradual recovery
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods do not outperform single-series trigger periods in mean 20-day forward return by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.10), invalidating the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis recovery regime where housing remains deeply depressed, any sustained uptick in HOUST signals incremental restoration of household balance sheets and construction activity, which disproportionately boosts risk appetite and drives S&P 500 forward returns higher over a quarter horizon; this effect is regime-specific and does not hold in normal-cycle housing markets.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 consecutive months from a depressed base (<650k units)
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 596.0 thousand units, near historic lows as of Jan 2011
failure_condition: HOUST upticks from depressed levels fail to precede positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of historical post-crisis analogues, or rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects sustained consumer spending momentum, which flows through to near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 387,123 million dollars, trending upward as of Feb 2011
failure_condition: Correlation between trailing RSAFS growth and next 5-day SPX return is not significantly positive (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling sample, or rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO crosses above its trailing 6-month average by a meaningful margin, it signals an inflection into expanding manufacturing output, which historically precedes upward earnings revisions for cyclical firms and drives S&P 500 gains over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO level crosses above its trailing 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.1452, recovering from post-recession trough
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following the crossing trigger are not significantly higher than unconditional baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05) across a 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of near-zero FEDFUNDS and low, stable CPIAUCSL inflation defines a low-rate, low-inflation regime in which the Fed has both room and rationale to maintain accommodation, extending the equity risk-premium compression beyond what either series predicts alone and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% and CPIAUCSL at 221.062 indicating subdued inflation with ultra-low rates
regime_hint: ZIRP with low and stable inflation
failure_condition: The joint-condition regime does not produce mean 60-day forward returns exceeding the unconditional historical mean by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.10), or the effect disappears once CPIAUCSL is controlled for independently.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month PAYEMS gain exceeding 150,000 jobs represents a threshold signal of labor market acceleration strong enough to shift near-term growth expectations, triggering a risk-on rally in the S&P 500 within the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS reported at 130515 thousand persons with gradual monthly gains through early 2011
failure_condition: Among months where PAYEMS gain exceeds 150,000, the following 5-day SPX return is positive in fewer than 55% of instances, or rank IC < 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.
